title: The Cabin Tradition
story_html:
Every summer, Ellie's family followed the same tradition: they drove west for two days to reach Grandma's cabin by a quiet creek. This summer vacation was Ellie's favorite week of the whole year, and the whole family called it their biggest event of the summer.

This year, her cousin Max came along. Usually a kind and cheerful boy, Max seemed unusually rude when he arrived, barely saying hello before disappearing into his room. Ellie felt hurt and confused.

That evening, Grandma called Ellie over for a quiet moment on the porch. Grandma was always wise and caring, so Ellie described what happened. "He's just nervous," Grandma said gently. "Middle school starts soon, and he's serious about not knowing anyone there." Grandma was patient, reminding Ellie that people act strange sometimes for real reasons underneath.

The next morning was windy, with leaves blowing across the yard. Ellie invited Max to walk with her along the slow-moving stream. At first he said little, but soon they began a guessing game about which fish lived under the rocks. Max started to smile.

They spent the afternoon studying the water together, watching small silver fish dart past. Max admitted he was certain he would have no friends when he got there, and it felt hard to say out loud. Ellie promised to call him every week once the term started, and told him that was one thing he could count on.

Before they had to finish their walk and head back, Dad snapped a photograph of the two of them by the water, laughing until their eyes were shut. Each year's picture was unique, since they never posed the same way twice.

That night, Ellie learned something new: even her best week of summer could make room for someone else's quiet worry. It became a memory she would keep for a long time.

patient.def: Able to wait calmly without getting angry.

memory.def: Something you remember from the past.

vacation.def: A trip or time away from school or work.

tradition.def: Something a family or group does the same way every year.

kind.def: Friendly, caring, and helpful to others.

guess.def: To give an answer without being sure.

learn.def: To gain new knowledge or a new skill.

serious.def: Not joking; thoughtful and thinking carefully about something.

wise.def: Able to make good decisions based on experience.

promise.def: To say for sure that you will do something.

slow.def: Not fast; taking a long time.

caring.def: Feeling and showing concern for other people.

event.def: Something that happens, especially something important.

describe.def: To say what something is like in words.

unique.def: Being the only one of its kind; very special.

hard.def: Difficult to do, or done with a lot of effort.

photograph.def: A picture taken with a camera.

finish.def: To complete something; to reach the end of it.

school.def: A place where children go to learn.

study.def: To spend time learning about something.

questions.qtext: In "Usually a kind and cheerful boy, Max seemed unusually rude when he arrived, barely saying hello", what does rude mean?
questions.explain: A — rude means not polite or respectful, which is why Max barely saying hello surprised Ellie.

questions.qtext: In "Grandma was patient, reminding Ellie that people act strange sometimes for real reasons underneath", what does real mean?
questions.explain: B — real means actually existing or genuine, meaning Max's odd behavior had a true cause behind it.

questions.qtext: In "Each year's picture was unique, since they never posed the same way twice", what does unique mean?
options: the only one of its kind
questions.explain: B — unique means one of a kind, showing that every year's family photograph came out a little different.

questions.qtext: Why was Max acting rude when he first arrived at Grandma's cabin?
options: He was angry at Ellie for something she did
He was nervous about starting middle school soon
He did not like fishing in the stream
He wanted to go home early
questions.explain: B — Grandma explained that Max was nervous because middle school was starting soon and he did not know anyone there.

questions.qtext: What did Ellie learn by the end of the story?
options: That fishing is more fun than taking photographs
That her favorite week could still make room for someone else's worries
That Grandma was too strict with Max
That she should stop visiting the cabin every summer
questions.explain: B — the story ends with Ellie realizing that even her best week of summer had space for Max's quiet worry.
